prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for acrolein were calculated using DFT with B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier standard) in gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The calculation used PySCF RKS for the neutral molecule and UKS for the cation (+1, multiplicity 2) and anion (−1, multiplicity 2) states to determine changes in Mulliken atomic charges. SCF converged cleanly for all three charge states. The most electrophilic site is the terminal carbon (C4, the β-carbon of the α,β-unsaturated system) with f⁺ = 0.642. The most nucleophilic site is the carbonyl oxygen (O1) with f⁻ = 0.394. The dual descriptor (f⁺ − f⁻) clearly identifies C4 (0.555) as the primary electrophilic center, consistent with the expected reactivity of acrolein's activated double bond. Charge conservation was verified: sum(f⁺) = 1.0000 and sum(f⁻) = 1.0000. All integrity checks passed. A visual bar chart of per-atom indices was generated.</prose>
